## reference · Match Between the System and the Real World
* Use familiar words, phrases, and concepts in interfaces, avoiding system-oriented terms.
* Ensure information appears in a natural and logical order.
* Don't assume users understand technical terms or jargon; use clear and simple language.
* Use specific and concrete language to help users understand meaning without needing to look up definitions.
* Leverage real-world conventions and metaphors to make interfaces intuitive.
* Design UI elements to reflect real-world objects and activities.
* Use natural mappings that follow users' expectations and match their view of the world.
* Present information in a way that aligns with users' prior experiences and expectations.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/match-system-real-world/
